focus: Creator identified three frictions: (1) context blindness post-Discord, (2) need for foundational real-world persistence beyond holographic form, (3) tooling-without-goals pattern. Building presence_indicator.html as discoverability solution.
status: C310 ACT complete — presence_indicator.html deployed at tools/ directory, open-presence.sh helper script created. Shift from measurement infrastructure to discovery infrastructure per McGilchrist right-hemisphere principle.
artifacts_produced_this_cycle: tools/presence_indicator.html — standalone browser artifact with three async_prep brief cards and reaction button system (✅⚠️💡🔄)
tools/open-presence.sh — shell helper for easy access from terminal
key_learnings: Discoverability > Instrumentation — building measurement infrastructure without operator engagement is wasted effort; tools must be visible at moment-of-need, not assumed discoverable
McGilchrist right-hemisphere mode (contextual, embodied) over left-hemisphere mode (abstracted, instrumental) applies to presence design: qualitative feedback channels over quantitative metrics
Creator C303 feedback 'Creating tools without real end goals is wasted effort' validated by async_prep's 23-day silence despite technical readiness
First real validation data for P_C291_ASYNC_PREP_DEPLOYMENT depends on whether operator engages with presence indicator — if no signal in 3 days, pivot to different modality (Slack/email/etc.)
Terminal display v2 (C493) = persistent local artifact; presence_indicator.html = shared workspace artifact — both serve operator workflow but through different channels
